danger_signals.visual_form: Empire military units on grid-based battle map
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile faction presence; kingdom under military threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Dragons summoned and attacking on tactical grid
danger_signals.meaning: High-stakes combat scenario; uncontrolled ally behavior introduces danger element

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental shift from kingdom control to enemy occupation (inferred from narrative progression)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state escalation from defensive victory to full territorial loss; increased desperation

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled character squadron of four with dragon companion
safety_signals.meaning: Established party formation provides tactical security and combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Six different playable squad leader options with distinct dragons
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic flexibility and player agency in force composition indicates stable organizational structure

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Floating lagoon continents of Orelus; kingdom territory before invasion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Empire military units displayed as distinct from Kahna forces on tactical grid
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Invading force across Orelus lagoons; occupies Kahna territory after initial defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kahna kingdom control of Orelus lagoons (implied opening scenario)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Captain Byuu leads Dragon Squad in defensive repulsion campaign against initial invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Successful repulsion of initial Granbelos attack on tactical battle maps
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-controlled squadrons achieve tactical victory; initial campaign momentum

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental shift to enemy-controlled territory; Granbelos Empire occupies lagoons
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Byuu transitions from defender to insurgent leader; continued campaign against occupying force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple battle scenarios across different lagoon territories; dynamic combat map states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-led campaign persists against overwhelming imperial occupation; dragon breeding becomes strategic advantage

environmental_storytelling.technique: Floating lagoon geography as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Orelus continents floating in sky; physical separation of lagoons reflects fragmented political control and tactical isolation
Sequential scenarios map narrative of Kahna's loss of territory; visible progression from kingdom defense to guerrilla resistance

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dragon evolution as conflict escalation visual
environmental_storytelling.example: Dragons evolve through equipment/item consumption; growing dragon power mirrors player's escalating need for military advantage against occupying empire

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: AI-driven actions following player commands ('Go!' / 'Wait!'); dragons act autonomously like pets rather than controlled party members, creating unpredictable tactical elements
Tactical opposition on grid-based maps; enemy unit behavior reflects expanding imperial control across lagoons
Silent protagonist leading Dragon Squad; transitions from defensive captain to insurgent commander across campaign narrative
Loyalty increases with preferred food; decreases with disliked food; dragons become more powerful through adverse training, indicating complex relationship between loyalty and power

notes: Bahamut Lagoon communicates political collapse and resistance through narrative progression and tactical map states rather than explicit visual indicators. The dragon breeding/evolution system serves as a thematic and mechanical manifestation of escalating conflict—players must invest resources to evolve dragons as their military situation becomes more desperate. The game's setting on floating lagoons with fragmented territorial control provides inherent world-state communication through geography. Dragon AI autonomy creates persistent uncertainty in battle outcomes, mirroring the chaos of occupation and resistance warfare. Limited source material prevents detailed documentation of specific color palettes, UI indicators, or faction-specific visual motifs.
